title: Dental Care for Veterans Act
plain_summary: The Dental Care for Veterans Act would let all veterans get dental care through the Department of Veterans Affairs, just like they can get other medical care. Right now, only some veterans can get dental care from the VA if they have certain dental problems from their military service or meet other special requirements. The new dental benefits would start over four years, with different groups of veterans becoming able to get care at different times based on things like their disability level or financial need. This would make dental care available to many more veterans than can get it now.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Dental Care for Veterans Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Requirement that Secretary of Veterans Affairs Furnish Dental Care in the Same Manner as Any Other Medical Service
sections.text: The Department of Veterans Affairs must provide dental care to veterans just like any other medical care. This means dental services will be treated the same way as regular doctor visits or hospital care. The changes will happen in stages over four years. Veterans who already get dental care through the VA will keep getting it right away when this law starts. Other veterans will become eligible for dental care based on their priority group. Veterans in priority groups one and two will get dental care one year after the law starts. Veterans in priority groups three and four will get dental care two years after the law starts. Veterans in priority groups five and six will get dental care three years after the law starts. Veterans in priority groups seven and eight will get dental care four years after the law starts. The law also makes technical changes to update references in the legal code and fix section headings to match the new dental care rules.
